summary:
The entire upper lip receives its sensory innervation primarily through branches originating from the maxillary division of the trigeminal nerve (Trigeminal Nerve V2).
Key supporting fact: This includes significant contribution via the infraorbital branch which supplies most areas including central part of both commissures up until about one-third above them along each side. Agent2 summarized that the primary supplier of sensation to the whole upper lip is the maxillary division of the trigeminal nerve (specifically mentioning the infraorbital branch) based on multiple reliable sources indicating extensive involvement in providing tactile information across nearly all parts of it. Key supportive detail noted was how far-reaching these nerves extend within the region defined anatomically around lips' top portion. 

Note: Removed redundant citations and detailed explanations while retaining core message intact focusing strictly per instruction requirements regarding brevity & relevance directly tied back to initial query posed without extraneous elaboration beyond necessary context provided initially herein.
